channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Anomalous Terahertz Nonlinearity in Disordered $s$-Wave Superconductor Close to the Superconductor-Insulator Transition
item.description:
Author(s): Hao Wang, Jiayu Yuan, Hongkai Shi, Haojie Li, Xiaoqing Jia, Xiaohui Song, Liyu Shi, Tianyi Wu, Li Yue, Yangmu Li, Kui Jin, Dong Wu, Jianlin Luo, Xinbo Wang, Tao Dong, and Nan-Lin Wang

A persistent normal-state third-harmonic generation (THG) signal extending up to ten times Tc and a multipeak THG spectrum in the superconducting state reveal an unexpected interplay between disorder, electronic correlations, and superconducting inhomogeneity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146004] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hao Wang, Jiayu Yuan, Hongkai Shi, Haojie Li, Xiaoqing Jia, Xiaohui Song, Liyu Shi, Tianyi Wu, Li Yue, Yangmu Li, Kui Jin, Dong Wu, Jianlin Luo, Xinbo Wang, Tao Dong, and Nan-Lin Wang

A persistent normal-state third-harmonic generation (THG) signal extending up to ten times Tc and a multipeak THG spectrum in the superconducting state reveal an unexpected interplay between disorder, electronic correlations, and superconducting inhomogeneity.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146004] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Anomalous Terahertz Nonlinearity in Disordered $s$-Wave Superconductor Close to the Superconductor-Insulator Transition
item.dc:creator: Hao Wang, Jiayu Yuan, Hongkai Shi, Haojie Li, Xiaoqing Jia, Xiaohui Song, Liyu Shi, Tianyi Wu, Li Yue, Yangmu Li, Kui Jin, Dong Wu, Jianlin Luo, Xinbo Wang, Tao Dong, and Nan-Lin Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Antiferromagnetic Quantum Critical Point and Superconductivity in Self-Intercalated TMD ${\mathrm{V}}_{1/4}{\mathrm{VS}}_{2}$ under High Pressure
item.description:
Author(s): Jingwei Miao, Jiajia Feng, Wei Zhong, Jianing Xu, Naohisa Hirao, Hirokazu Kadobayashi, Sheng Jiang, Fang Chen, Bin Chen, and Binbin Yue

Self-intercalation in transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) offers a unique strategy for doping and spin ordering that preserves structural integrity, minimizing lattice distortions. This modification introduces additional electron density, spin states, and potentially spontaneous superlattice for…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146501] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingwei Miao, Jiajia Feng, Wei Zhong, Jianing Xu, Naohisa Hirao, Hirokazu Kadobayashi, Sheng Jiang, Fang Chen, Bin Chen, and Binbin Yue

Self-intercalation in transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs) offers a unique strategy for doping and spin ordering that preserves structural integrity, minimizing lattice distortions. This modification introduces additional electron density, spin states, and potentially spontaneous superlattice for…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146501] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Antiferromagnetic Quantum Critical Point and Superconductivity in Self-Intercalated TMD ${\mathrm{V}}_{1/4}{\mathrm{VS}}_{2}$ under High Pressure
item.dc:creator: Jingwei Miao, Jiajia Feng, Wei Zhong, Jianing Xu, Naohisa Hirao, Hirokazu Kadobayashi, Sheng Jiang, Fang Chen, Bin Chen, and Binbin Yue
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Altermagnetic Polarons: The Fate of Altermagnetic Band Splittings at Strong Coupling
item.description:
Author(s): Maria Daghofer, Krzysztof Wohlfeld, and Jeroen van den Brink

While a spin-dependent band splitting is one of the characteristic features of altermagnets, the conventional band picture itself breaks down in the many altermagnets that are correlated Mott materials. We employ two numerical many-body methods, the self-consistent Born approximation and a variation…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146502] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Maria Daghofer, Krzysztof Wohlfeld, and Jeroen van den Brink

While a spin-dependent band splitting is one of the characteristic features of altermagnets, the conventional band picture itself breaks down in the many altermagnets that are correlated Mott materials. We employ two numerical many-body methods, the self-consistent Born approximation and a variation…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146502] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnetic Polarons: The Fate of Altermagnetic Band Splittings at Strong Coupling
item.dc:creator: Maria Daghofer, Krzysztof Wohlfeld, and Jeroen van den Brink
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Microscopic Phase-Transition Theory of Charge Density Waves: Revealing Hidden Crossovers of Phason and Amplitudon
item.description:
Author(s): F. Yang and L. Q. Chen

We develop a self-consistent phase-transition theory of charge density waves (CDWs), starting from a purely microscopic model. Specifically, we derive a microscopic CDW gap equation $|{\mathrm{Δ}}_{0}(T)|$, taking into account the thermal phase fluctuations (i.e., thermal excitation of phason) and t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146503] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Yang and L. Q. Chen

We develop a self-consistent phase-transition theory of charge density waves (CDWs), starting from a purely microscopic model. Specifically, we derive a microscopic CDW gap equation |Δ0(T)|, taking into account the thermal phase fluctuations (i.e., thermal excitation of phason) and their influence o…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146503] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Microscopic Phase-Transition Theory of Charge Density Waves: Revealing Hidden Crossovers of Phason and Amplitudon
item.dc:creator: F. Yang and L. Q. Chen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Dirac Node Pinning from Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya Interactions in a Kagome Spin Liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Ajesh Kumar, Byungmin Kang, and Patrick A. Lee

Recent experiments on the Kagome spin liquid candidate material ${\mathrm{YCu}}_{3}(\mathrm{OH}{)}_{6}{\mathrm{Br}}_{2}[{\mathrm{Br}}_{1−y}(\mathrm{OH}{)}_{y}]$ suggest the presence of Dirac fermionic spinons near the magnetization plateau at $1/9$. Theories suggest that the spinons are charge neutr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146704] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ajesh Kumar, Byungmin Kang, and Patrick A. Lee

Recent experiments on the Kagome spin liquid candidate material YCu3(OH)6Br2[Br1−y(OH)y] suggest the presence of Dirac fermionic spinons near the magnetization plateau at 1/9. Theories suggest that the spinons are charge neutral spin-1/2 excitations, in a 2π/3 flux, which triples the unit cell. Gene…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146704] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Dirac Node Pinning from Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya Interactions in a Kagome Spin Liquid
item.dc:creator: Ajesh Kumar, Byungmin Kang, and Patrick A. Lee
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Effective Theory of Ultrafast Skyrmion Nucleation
item.description:
Author(s): Rein Liefferink, Lukas Körber, Kathinka Gerlinger, Bastian Pfau, Felix Büttner, and Johan H. Mentink

Laser-induced ultrafast skyrmion nucleation has been experimentally demonstrated in several materials. So far, atomistic models have been used to corroborate experimental results. However, such simulations do not provide a simple intuitive understanding of the underlying physics. Here, we propose a …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146705] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rein Liefferink, Lukas Körber, Kathinka Gerlinger, Bastian Pfau, Felix Büttner, and Johan H. Mentink

Laser-induced ultrafast skyrmion nucleation has been experimentally demonstrated in several materials. So far, atomistic models have been used to corroborate experimental results. However, such simulations do not provide a simple intuitive understanding of the underlying physics. Here, we propose a …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146705] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Effective Theory of Ultrafast Skyrmion Nucleation
item.dc:creator: Rein Liefferink, Lukas Körber, Kathinka Gerlinger, Bastian Pfau, Felix Büttner, and Johan H. Mentink
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Cavity-Induced Coherent Magnetization and Polaritons in Altermagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Libor Šmejkal, and James K. Freericks

Altermagnets feature antiparallel spin sublattices with $d$-, $g$-, or $i$-wave spin order, yielding nonrelativistic spin splitting without net magnetization. In this Letter, we embed a two-dimensional $d$-wave altermagnet in a driven terahertz cavity. Because the intrinsic altermagnetic texture re…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146904] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Libor Šmejkal, and James K. Freericks

Altermagnets feature antiparallel spin sublattices with d-, g-, or i-wave spin order, yielding nonrelativistic spin splitting without net magnetization. In this Letter, we embed a two-dimensional d-wave altermagnet in a driven terahertz cavity. Because the intrinsic altermagnetic texture renders th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146904] Published Fri Apr 10, 2026
item.dc:title: Cavity-Induced Coherent Magnetization and Polaritons in Altermagnets
item.dc:creator: Mohsen Yarmohammadi, Libor Šmejkal, and James K. Freericks
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Bloch Diode
item.description:
Author(s): M. Houzet, T. Vakhtel, and J. S. Meyer

The Josephson diode effect—an asymmetry of critical currents at opposite polarities—has attracted much attention recently. One of its simplest realizations is an asymmetric SQUID with multiple Josephson harmonics tuned away from half-integer flux (in units of the superconducting flux quantum). Here …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146001] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Houzet, T. Vakhtel, and J. S. Meyer

The Josephson diode effect—an asymmetry of critical currents at opposite polarities—has attracted much attention recently. One of its simplest realizations is an asymmetric SQUID with multiple Josephson harmonics tuned away from half-integer flux (in units of the superconducting flux quantum). Here …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146001] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Bloch Diode
item.dc:creator: M. Houzet, T. Vakhtel, and J. S. Meyer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Friedel Oscillations in Nanoconfined $^{4}\mathrm{He}$
item.description:
Author(s): Bernd Rosenow and Adrian Del Maestro

One-dimensional bosonic systems, such as helium confined to nanopores, exhibit Luttinger liquid behavior characterized by density waves as collective excitations. We investigate the impact of a scattering potential on a low-dimensional quantum liquid. We consider a microscopic model of $^{4}\mathrm{…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146002] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bernd Rosenow and Adrian Del Maestro

One-dimensional bosonic systems, such as helium confined to nanopores, exhibit Luttinger liquid behavior characterized by density waves as collective excitations. We investigate the impact of a scattering potential on a low-dimensional quantum liquid. We consider a microscopic model of He4 inside a …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146002] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Friedel Oscillations in Nanoconfined $^{4}\mathrm{He}$
item.dc:creator: Bernd Rosenow and Adrian Del Maestro
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Resonant Magnetophonon Emission by Supersonic Electrons in Ultrahigh-Mobility Two-Dimensional Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Z. T. Wang, M. Hilke, N. Fong, D. G. Austing, S. A. Studenikin, K. W. West, and L. N. Pfeiffer

We investigate resonant acoustic phonon scattering in the magnetoresistivity of an ultrahigh-mobility two-dimensional electron gas system subject to DC current in the temperature range 10 mK to 3.9 K. For a DC current density of $∼1.1\text{ }\text{ }\mathrm{A}/\mathrm{m}$, the induced carrier drift …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146302] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Z. T. Wang, M. Hilke, N. Fong, D. G. Austing, S. A. Studenikin, K. W. West, and L. N. Pfeiffer

We investigate resonant acoustic phonon scattering in the magnetoresistivity of an ultrahigh-mobility two-dimensional electron gas system subject to DC current in the temperature range 10 mK to 3.9 K. For a DC current density of ∼1.1  A/m, the induced carrier drift velocity vdrift becomes equal to t…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146302] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Resonant Magnetophonon Emission by Supersonic Electrons in Ultrahigh-Mobility Two-Dimensional Systems
item.dc:creator: Z. T. Wang, M. Hilke, N. Fong, D. G. Austing, S. A. Studenikin, K. W. West, and L. N. Pfeiffer
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Impurity Sensing of Altermagnetic Order
item.description:
Author(s): V. A. S. V. Bittencourt, Hossein Hosseinabadi, Jairo Sinova, Libor Šmejkal, and Jamir Marino

Quantum sensing with individual spin defects has emerged as a versatile platform to probe microscopic properties of condensed matter systems. Here we demonstrate that quantum relaxometry with nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond can reveal the anisotropic spin dynamics of altermagnetic insulator…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146701] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. A. S. V. Bittencourt, Hossein Hosseinabadi, Jairo Sinova, Libor Šmejkal, and Jamir Marino

Quantum sensing with individual spin defects has emerged as a versatile platform to probe microscopic properties of condensed matter systems. Here we demonstrate that quantum relaxometry with nitrogen-vacancy (NV) centers in diamond can reveal the anisotropic spin dynamics of altermagnetic insulator…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146701] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Impurity Sensing of Altermagnetic Order
item.dc:creator: V. A. S. V. Bittencourt, Hossein Hosseinabadi, Jairo Sinova, Libor Šmejkal, and Jamir Marino
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Finite-Size Spectral Signatures of Order by Quantum Disorder: A Perspective from Anderson’s Tower of States
item.description:
Author(s): Subhankar Khatua, Griffin C. Howson, Michel J. P. Gingras, and Jeffrey G. Rau

Order by quantum disorder leaves distinct finite-size signatures in the exact diagonalization spectrum that are captured by an effective quantum-rotor description analogous to the Anderson tower of states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146702] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Subhankar Khatua, Griffin C. Howson, Michel J. P. Gingras, and Jeffrey G. Rau

Order by quantum disorder leaves distinct finite-size signatures in the exact diagonalization spectrum that are captured by an effective quantum-rotor description analogous to the Anderson tower of states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146702] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Finite-Size Spectral Signatures of Order by Quantum Disorder: A Perspective from Anderson’s Tower of States
item.dc:creator: Subhankar Khatua, Griffin C. Howson, Michel J. P. Gingras, and Jeffrey G. Rau
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Strongly Nonlinear Nanocavity Exciton Polaritons in Gate-Tunable Monolayer Semiconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Zhi Wang, Bumho Kim, Bo Zhen, and Li He

Precise adjustment of the doping level in a transition metal dichalcogenide heterostructure using electrostatic gating demonstrates a wide range of tunability in the exciton oscillator strength and thus the exciton photon hybridization.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146901] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhi Wang, Bumho Kim, Bo Zhen, and Li He

Precise adjustment of the doping level in a transition metal dichalcogenide heterostructure using electrostatic gating demonstrates a wide range of tunability in the exciton oscillator strength and thus the exciton photon hybridization.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146901] Published Wed Apr 08, 2026
item.dc:title: Strongly Nonlinear Nanocavity Exciton Polaritons in Gate-Tunable Monolayer Semiconductors
item.dc:creator: Zhi Wang, Bumho Kim, Bo Zhen, and Li He
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnetoresistance Oscillations in Few-Layer ${\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$ in Superconducting Fluctuation Regime
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaolong Yin, Congzhe Cao, Yibin Feng, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Jiawei Mei, Qi-Kun Xue, and Shuo-Ying Yang

The observation of magnetoresistance oscillation in NbSe2 thin films, reminiscent of Little-Parks oscillations, is attributed to superconducting phase fluctuations unlike other previously reported oscillations.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146301] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaolong Yin, Congzhe Cao, Yibin Feng, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Jiawei Mei, Qi-Kun Xue, and Shuo-Ying Yang

The observation of magnetoresistance oscillation in NbSe2 thin films, reminiscent of Little-Parks oscillations, is attributed to superconducting phase fluctuations unlike other previously reported oscillations.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146301] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnetoresistance Oscillations in Few-Layer ${\mathrm{NbSe}}_{2}$ in Superconducting Fluctuation Regime
item.dc:creator: Xiaolong Yin, Congzhe Cao, Yibin Feng, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Jiawei Mei, Qi-Kun Xue, and Shuo-Ying Yang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Collective Interference of Phonon Spin and Dipole Moment Rotation Induced Circular Dichroism
item.description:
Author(s): Yizhou Liu, Yu-Tao Tan, Dapeng Liu, and Jie Ren

The classical field description of “phonon spin” relies on the invariance of a continuous elastic field under infinitesimal rotation. However, a local “medium element” in the continuous field may contain large numbers of vibrational particles at microscopic level, like for complex lattices with many…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146601] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yizhou Liu, Yu-Tao Tan, Dapeng Liu, and Jie Ren

The classical field description of “phonon spin” relies on the invariance of a continuous elastic field under infinitesimal rotation. However, a local “medium element” in the continuous field may contain large numbers of vibrational particles at microscopic level, like for complex lattices with many…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146601] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Collective Interference of Phonon Spin and Dipole Moment Rotation Induced Circular Dichroism
item.dc:creator: Yizhou Liu, Yu-Tao Tan, Dapeng Liu, and Jie Ren
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Parity Anomalous Semimetal with Minimal Conductivity Induced by an In-Plane Magnetic Field
item.description:
Author(s): Binbin Wang, Jiayuan Hu, Bo Fu, Jiaqi Li, Yunchuan Kong, Kai-Zhi Bai, Shun-Qing Shen, and Di Xiao

The interplay between topological materials and local symmetry breaking yields diverse topological quantum phenomena. A notable example is the parity-anomalous semimetal (PAS), which hosts a single unpaired gapless Dirac cone with a half-integer quantized Hall conductivity. Here, we realize this pha…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146602] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Binbin Wang, Jiayuan Hu, Bo Fu, Jiaqi Li, Yunchuan Kong, Kai-Zhi Bai, Shun-Qing Shen, and Di Xiao

The interplay between topological materials and local symmetry breaking yields diverse topological quantum phenomena. A notable example is the parity-anomalous semimetal (PAS), which hosts a single unpaired gapless Dirac cone with a half-integer quantized Hall conductivity. Here, we realize this pha…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 146602] Published Mon Apr 06, 2026
item.dc:title: Parity Anomalous Semimetal with Minimal Conductivity Induced by an In-Plane Magnetic Field
item.dc:creator: Binbin Wang, Jiayuan Hu, Bo Fu, Jiaqi Li, Yunchuan Kong, Kai-Zhi Bai, Shun-Qing Shen, and Di Xiao
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Nanoscale Imaging of Magnetotransport around a Circular $p\text{−}n$ Junction in Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Zachary J. Krebs, Wyatt A. Behn, Keenan J. Smith, Margaret A. Fortman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pathak S. Parashar, Michael M. Fogler, and Victor W. Brar

Electrons in graphene follow various spiraling paths when they flow around a circular barrier under the influence of a magnetic field.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136301] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zachary J. Krebs, Wyatt A. Behn, Keenan J. Smith, Margaret A. Fortman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pathak S. Parashar, Michael M. Fogler, and Victor W. Brar

Electrons in graphene follow various spiraling paths when they flow around a circular barrier under the influence of a magnetic field.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136301] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Nanoscale Imaging of Magnetotransport around a Circular $p\text{−}n$ Junction in Graphene
item.dc:creator: Zachary J. Krebs, Wyatt A. Behn, Keenan J. Smith, Margaret A. Fortman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pathak S. Parashar, Michael M. Fogler, and Victor W. Brar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electrically Driven Plasmon-Polaritonic Bistability in Dirac Electron Tunneling Transistors
item.description:
Author(s): Shuai Zhang, Yang Xu, Junhe Zhang, Dihao Sun, Yinan Dong, Matthew Fu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Cory Dean, Monica Allen, Jeffery Allen, F. Javier García de Abajo, Antti J. Moilanen, Lukas Novotny, and D. N. Basov

An electrically driven nonlinear mechanism based on resonant tunneling, plasmon-polaritonic bistability, unlocks the potential realization of polaritonic on-chip optical memory and switching.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136904] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuai Zhang, Yang Xu, Junhe Zhang, Dihao Sun, Yinan Dong, Matthew Fu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Cory Dean, Monica Allen, Jeffery Allen, F. Javier García de Abajo, Antti J. Moilanen, Lukas Novotny, and D. N. Basov

An electrically driven nonlinear mechanism based on resonant tunneling, plasmon-polaritonic bistability, unlocks the potential realization of polaritonic on-chip optical memory and switching.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136904] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrically Driven Plasmon-Polaritonic Bistability in Dirac Electron Tunneling Transistors
item.dc:creator: Shuai Zhang, Yang Xu, Junhe Zhang, Dihao Sun, Yinan Dong, Matthew Fu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Cory Dean, Monica Allen, Jeffery Allen, F. Javier García de Abajo, Antti J. Moilanen, Lukas Novotny, and D. N. Basov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: High Compression Blue-Detuned Magneto-Optical Trap of Polyatomic Molecules
item.description:
Author(s): Christian Hallas, Grace K. Li, Nathaniel B. Vilas, Paige Robichaud, Loïc Anderegg, and John M. Doyle

Researchers have improved trapping of polyatomic molecules while also controlling their collisions—two important advances for ultracold polyatomic molecular physics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133402] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Christian Hallas, Grace K. Li, Nathaniel B. Vilas, Paige Robichaud, Loïc Anderegg, and John M. Doyle

Researchers have improved trapping of polyatomic molecules while also controlling their collisions—two important advances for ultracold polyatomic molecular physics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133402] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.dc:title: High Compression Blue-Detuned Magneto-Optical Trap of Polyatomic Molecules
item.dc:creator: Christian Hallas, Grace K. Li, Nathaniel B. Vilas, Paige Robichaud, Loïc Anderegg, and John M. Doyle
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Quantum Critical Dynamics Induced by Topological Zero Modes
item.description:
Author(s): Ilia Komissarov, Tobias Holder, and Raquel Queiroz

We investigate low-frequency ac transport in the Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain with chiral disorder near the topological delocalization transition. Our key finding is that the formation of hybridized pairs of topological domain wall zero modes leads to the anomalous logarithmic scaling of the ac …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136602] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ilia Komissarov, Tobias Holder, and Raquel Queiroz

We investigate low-frequency ac transport in the Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain with chiral disorder near the topological delocalization transition. Our key finding is that the formation of hybridized pairs of topological domain wall zero modes leads to the anomalous logarithmic scaling of the ac …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136602] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Critical Dynamics Induced by Topological Zero Modes
item.dc:creator: Ilia Komissarov, Tobias Holder, and Raquel Queiroz
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Exact Duality at Low Energy in a Josephson Tunnel Junction Coupled to a Transmission Line
item.description:
Author(s): Luca Giacomelli, Michel H. Devoret, and Cristiano Ciuti

We theoretically explore the low-energy behavior of a Josephson tunnel junction coupled to a finite-length, charge-biased transmission line and compare it to its flux-biased counterpart. For transmission lines of increasing length, we show that the low-energy charge-dependent energy bands of the cha…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 130401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luca Giacomelli, Michel H. Devoret, and Cristiano Ciuti

We theoretically explore the low-energy behavior of a Josephson tunnel junction coupled to a finite-length, charge-biased transmission line and compare it to its flux-biased counterpart. For transmission lines of increasing length, we show that the low-energy charge-dependent energy bands of the cha…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 130401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Exact Duality at Low Energy in a Josephson Tunnel Junction Coupled to a Transmission Line
item.dc:creator: Luca Giacomelli, Michel H. Devoret, and Cristiano Ciuti
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Angular Momentum Fluctuations in the Phonon Vacuum of Symmetric Crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Rule Yi, Violet Williams, and Benedetta Flebus

Although time-reversal and inversion symmetry constrain the angular momentum of each phonon mode to vanish, we show that the vacuum state of crystals with such symmetries can, nevertheless, exhibit finite angular momentum fluctuations, which persist at finite temperature. These fluctuations arise fr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136101] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rule Yi, Violet Williams, and Benedetta Flebus

Although time-reversal and inversion symmetry constrain the angular momentum of each phonon mode to vanish, we show that the vacuum state of crystals with such symmetries can, nevertheless, exhibit finite angular momentum fluctuations, which persist at finite temperature. These fluctuations arise fr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136101] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Angular Momentum Fluctuations in the Phonon Vacuum of Symmetric Crystals
item.dc:creator: Rule Yi, Violet Williams, and Benedetta Flebus
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Soft Phonon Charge-Density-Wave Formation in the Kagome Metal ${\mathrm{KV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, Chenchao Xu, Zhimian Wu, Huachen Rao, Zhaoyang Shan, Yi Liu, Guanghan Cao, Michael Smidman, Ming Shi, Huiqiu Yuan, Tao Wu, Xianhui Chen, Chao Cao, and Yu Song

A range of unusual emergent behaviors have been reported in the charge-density-wave (CDW) state of the $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K}$, Rb, Cs) kagome metals, including a CDW formation process without soft phonons, which points to an unconventional CDW mechanism. Here, we use in…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, Chenchao Xu, Zhimian Wu, Huachen Rao, Zhaoyang Shan, Yi Liu, Guanghan Cao, Michael Smidman, Ming Shi, Huiqiu Yuan, Tao Wu, Xianhui Chen, Chao Cao, and Yu Song

A range of unusual emergent behaviors have been reported in the charge-density-wave (CDW) state of the AV3Sb5 (A=K, Rb, Cs) kagome metals, including a CDW formation process without soft phonons, which points to an unconventional CDW mechanism. Here, we use inelastic x-ray scattering to show that the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Soft Phonon Charge-Density-Wave Formation in the Kagome Metal ${\mathrm{KV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Yifan Wang, Chenchao Xu, Zhimian Wu, Huachen Rao, Zhaoyang Shan, Yi Liu, Guanghan Cao, Michael Smidman, Ming Shi, Huiqiu Yuan, Tao Wu, Xianhui Chen, Chao Cao, and Yu Song
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fractional Quantum Multiferroics from Coupling of Fractional Quantum Ferroelectricity and Altermagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): M. Q. Dong, B. Liu, Z. H. Dai, Zhi-Xin Guo, Hongjun Xiang, and Xin-Gao Gong

Introducing altermagnetism in fractional quantum ferroelectrics yields magnetoelectric coupling that can switch altermagnetic splitting without rotation of the Néel vector via fractional displacements.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136702] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Q. Dong, B. Liu, Z. H. Dai, Zhi-Xin Guo, Hongjun Xiang, and Xin-Gao Gong

Introducing altermagnetism in fractional quantum ferroelectrics yields magnetoelectric coupling that can switch altermagnetic splitting without rotation of the Néel vector via fractional displacements.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136702] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractional Quantum Multiferroics from Coupling of Fractional Quantum Ferroelectricity and Altermagnetism
item.dc:creator: M. Q. Dong, B. Liu, Z. H. Dai, Zhi-Xin Guo, Hongjun Xiang, and Xin-Gao Gong
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Emergence of a Fluctuating Ground State in Y-Kapellasite under Pressure
item.description:
Author(s): Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal

Hydrostatic pressure drives a magnetically ordered kagome material into a fluctuating ground state that shares key features with quantum spin liquids.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136701] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal

Hydrostatic pressure drives a magnetically ordered kagome material into a fluctuating ground state that shares key features with quantum spin liquids.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136701] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergence of a Fluctuating Ground State in Y-Kapellasite under Pressure
item.dc:creator: Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Charge-Density Ripples Modulated by Nuclear Quantum Effects in High-Harmonic Generation in Solids
item.description:
Author(s): Shi-Qi Hu, Qing Chen, and Sheng Meng

Nuclear quantum effects (NQEs) significantly influence many fundamental physical and chemical phenomena. However, their impact on ultrafast dynamics remains poorly understood. Here, we demonstrate that solid-state high-harmonic generation (HHG) provides a promising platform for probing NQEs in attos…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136901] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shi-Qi Hu, Qing Chen, and Sheng Meng

Nuclear quantum effects (NQEs) significantly influence many fundamental physical and chemical phenomena. However, their impact on ultrafast dynamics remains poorly understood. Here, we demonstrate that solid-state high-harmonic generation (HHG) provides a promising platform for probing NQEs in attos…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136901] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Charge-Density Ripples Modulated by Nuclear Quantum Effects in High-Harmonic Generation in Solids
item.dc:creator: Shi-Qi Hu, Qing Chen, and Sheng Meng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Antisymmetric Raman Response
item.description:
Author(s): Mattia Udina and Indranil Paul

We develop the theory of antisymmetric Raman response, defined as the difference between the Raman signals of two scattering geometries related by an exchange of mutually perpendicular incoming and the outgoing photon polarizations. Such responses, finite in orthorhombic or lower symmetry systems, a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126505] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mattia Udina and Indranil Paul

We develop the theory of antisymmetric Raman response, defined as the difference between the Raman signals of two scattering geometries related by an exchange of mutually perpendicular incoming and the outgoing photon polarizations. Such responses, finite in orthorhombic or lower symmetry systems, a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126505] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Antisymmetric Raman Response
item.dc:creator: Mattia Udina and Indranil Paul
